_readme.purpose: MyKanban board data file. AIs can read and write this file directly to manage the board.

structure.meta: Version info and timestamps — update 'lastModified' (ISO 8601) on every write.
structure.board: Single board definition: id (string) + name (display name).
structure.columns: Ordered list of Kanban columns. Each column has: id (unique string), title (display name), order (0-based integer), color (hex #RRGGBB), wipLimit (optional number: max tickets before warning, omit for unlimited).
structure.tickets: Flat list of ALL tickets across all columns. Each ticket belongs to a column via 'columnId'.

ticket_fields.id: Unique string, e.g. 'ticket-abc123'. Must be unique across all tickets.
ticket_fields.columnId: Must match one of the 'id' values in the 'columns' array. Moving a ticket = changing this value.
ticket_fields.ticketNumber: Random 4-digit integer (1000–9999), displayed as 'TK-XXXX'. Set once on creation, never change.
ticket_fields.title: Short ticket title (required, non-empty string).
ticket_fields.description: Optional Markdown-formatted description. Use \n for newlines.
ticket_fields.labels: Optional array of strings, e.g. ['bug', 'frontend']. Use existing labels or create new ones freely.
ticket_fields.order: 0-based integer position within the column. Tickets in a column are sorted ascending by this value. Reorder by adjusting 'order' values of affected tickets.
ticket_fields.createdAt: ISO 8601 timestamp, set once on creation.
ticket_fields.updatedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp, update on every change to the ticket.

ai_rules: Always keep 'order' values consistent: no gaps required, but values must be unique within a column.
When moving a ticket to another column: change 'columnId' and set a new 'order' at the target position, reorder others if needed.
When creating a ticket: generate a unique 'id', pick a random 'ticketNumber' (1000–9999) not yet in use, set 'createdAt' and 'updatedAt' to now.
Always update 'meta.lastModified' to the current UTC time after any change.
Never delete or rename existing column IDs — use them as stable references.
The full schema is defined in 'kanban.schema.json' next to this file.
